trans-unit.target: Search analyses by title, description, or tag…
Try a different search term or category.
Configure dimensions and metrics in one form, then run a custom analysis.
Sign in to save your own analysis views.
Configure dimensions, metrics, and chart display in one place, then open the analysis.
Same controls as the analysis explorer, grouped for a custom run from the library.
Choose the main grouping for rows and buckets, and optionally add a series dimension to split the chart.
Select values for the table. Count is always included. Some metrics require a series dimension.
Which metric drives the chart bars or lines. Other selected metrics appear in the table.
Review your choices and open the analysis with the selected configuration.
What do you want to analyze?
No rows with known age for this selection; box plot is not shown.
Age distribution by category (min, quartiles, median, max)
Detailed cohort distribution (mean age & box plot)
Statistics → Distribution → Age cohorts: table, filters, and box plot by category.
The projection has no rows yet. Import data and rebuild the allocation stats projection to see charts here.
Select an import to view allocation distributions from the projection.
Aggregated across the full allocation stats projection: %count% rows total.
The selected state does not have enough hospitals in the statistics data. Showing public statistics instead.
The selected dispatch area does not have enough hospitals in the statistics data. Showing public statistics instead.
The selected cohort has fewer than 2 participating hospitals. Showing public statistics instead.
The “virtual remainder” assumes cases without any listed flag; overlapping flags can make segment sums exceed the total, so stacked percentages may exceed 100%.
Detailed KPIs, distributions and top lists for your hospitals will appear here in a future release.
{user, number, integer} Allocations (of {total, number, integer}, {percent, number, :: .#}%)
{percent, number, :: .#}% of {total, number, integer} allocations in this period
Showing all hospitals — no hospitals are assigned to your account.
Table widgets will be available here later.
Aggregated across all hospitals in the selected scope.
Not enough cases for a reliable baseline comparison.
Longer periods may reveal seasonal patterns.
Case volume changed by {percent} compared to the previous period.
{indication} cases changed by {percent} compared to the previous period.
No notable findings for the current selection.
Some dimensions (hospital, state, dispatch area) are only available in the matching scope filter above.
Select values for the table. Count is always included. Some metrics require a series dimension.
Which metric drives the chart bars or lines. Other selected metrics appear in the table.
No data for the selected scope and period.
No chart data for the selected scope and period.
Charts are not available for this numeric dimension. Use the table below.
Histogram charts are not yet supported. Use the table below.
Heatmap charts for hour and weekday are not yet available. Use the table below.
Many series — the legend may be hard to read.
Many categories — consider a horizontal bar chart.
Chart shows the top {limit} values; remaining values are grouped as “Other”.
Type a name or code to search, then select an indication to open its profile.
{count, number} cases in the selected scope and period
Compare two indications in the same scope
No notable differences between these two indications in the selected scope.
One or both indications have very few cases in this scope. Interpret differences with caution.
Select two indications or groups to compare their profiles side by side. Groups are marked with [Group] after the name.
Please select two subjects to compare.
Please select two different subjects to compare.
The selected group has no indications assigned yet.
These subjects share one or more indications. Allocations for shared indications are counted on both sides.
Physician escort occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Physician escort rate is in line between the two indications ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Physician escort occurs less often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Resuscitation room admission occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Resuscitation room admission is lower for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Cath lab indication occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Cath lab indication is lower for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Emergency urgency occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Emergency urgency is lower for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Patients aged 80+ occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Patients aged 80+ are less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Night cases occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Night cases are less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Weekend cases occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Weekend cases are less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Infectious patients occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Infectious patients are less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
CPR occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
CPR is less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Ventilation occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Ventilation is less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Shock occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Shock is less common for indication A ({percentA, number}% vs. {percentB, number}%).
Indication A has a markedly higher median age ({percentA, number} vs. {percentB, number} years).
Indication A has a markedly lower median age ({percentA, number} vs. {percentB, number} years).
Median transport time is longer for indication A ({percentA, number} vs. {percentB, number} minutes).
Median transport time is shorter for indication A ({percentA, number} vs. {percentB, number} minutes).
This group has no indications assigned yet.
No indication groups have been set up yet. Administrators can create groups in the admin area.
Not enough data to generate insights for this indication.
Night shift: 22:00–05:59 · Early shift: 06:00–13:59 · Late shift: 14:00–21:59
Physician escort occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Resuscitation room admission occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Cath lab indication occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Night cases occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Patients aged 80+ occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Emergency urgency occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Weekend cases occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Infectious patients occur {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
CPR occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Ventilation occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Shock occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}%).
Male patients are overrepresented ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}% among other indications).
Female patients are overrepresented ({indicationPercent, number}% vs. {baselinePercent, number}% among other indications).
Median age is markedly lower than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number} vs. {baselinePercent, number} years).
Median age is markedly higher than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number} vs. {baselinePercent, number} years).
Median transport time is longer than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number} vs. {baselinePercent, number} minutes).
Median transport time is shorter than for other indications ({indicationPercent, number} vs. {baselinePercent, number} minutes).
Configure scope and period for both sides, then apply to reload the comparison.
Not enough data in one or both scopes to generate reliable insights. Try a broader scope or period.
Colors show the relative share difference between scopes (Δ in %), not absolute case counts. Red indicates that primary is higher, while green indicates that primary is lower than comparison.
Physician escort occurs {ratio, number, :: .#}× more often ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Physician escort rate is in line with the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Physician escort occurs less often than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Resuscitation room rate is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Resuscitation room rate is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Cath lab rate is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Cath lab rate is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Emergency urgency is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Emergency urgency is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Share aged 80+ is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Share aged 80+ is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Night share is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Night share is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Weekend share is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Weekend share is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
CPR rate is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
CPR rate is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Ventilation rate is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Ventilation rate is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Shock rate is {ratio, number, :: .#}× higher ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Shock rate is lower than in the comparison ({primaryPercent, number}% vs. {comparisonPercent, number}%).
Gender distribution is in line with the comparison.
Median age is markedly lower ({primaryValue, number} vs. {comparisonValue, number} years).
Median age is markedly higher ({primaryValue, number} vs. {comparisonValue, number} years).
Median transport time is longer ({primaryValue, number} vs. {comparisonValue, number} minutes).
Median transport time is shorter ({primaryValue, number} vs. {comparisonValue, number} minutes).
No data available for this selection.
Gray areas indicate suppressed values due to small case counts.
Care is predominantly regional: {share, number}% of cases are treated within the origin dispatch area.
High centralization: {share, number}% of cases are treated in full-tier facilities.
Above-average transport times (mean {mean, number} min vs. {baselineMean, number} min baseline).
Emergency cases are geographically concentrated: {share, number}% originate from {origin}.
Notable deviations from the hospital base
Deployment statistics are well interpretable. Participating hospitals adequately cover the selected hospital base.
Deployment statistics are only moderately interpretable for the selected scope and period.
Deployment statistics are only limited interpretable because several relevant subgroups are weakly populated.
Deployment statistics are barely reliable because coverage is low ({percentage}%).
Deployment statistics are barely reliable because coverage is low and participating hospitals differ strongly from the hospital base.
Deployment statistics are limited interpretable because participating hospitals differ from the hospital base (deviation {difference}).
Deployment statistics are limited interpretable because many hospitals have too few cases ({share}% with ≥ {threshold}).
Limited reliability due to small hospital base
Compares the distribution of participating hospitals with the full hospital base across size, care level, urbanity and district. A lower difference means the sample is more representative.

Checks relevant hospital subgroups (size × care level and urbanity × care level) for sufficient participation. A subgroup counts as supported when at least {minParticipants} hospitals contribute data, or all hospitals in that subgroup when fewer than {minParticipants} exist. With fewer than 5 populated subgroups, the rating uses the share of supported subgroups.
How many participating hospitals fall into each range of allocations for this indication in the selected scope and period. Ranges from {threshold} cases upward count as sufficient case volume.
Overview of all hospitals in the dataset, participant coverage, and the allocation data basis.
Positive delta indicates overrepresentation of participants; negative delta indicates underrepresentation.
Marker size is proportional to bed count

The shaded regions show regional coverage: the share of participating hospitals in each dispatch area relative to all hospitals in that area. Red indicates low coverage, yellow moderate coverage, and green high coverage.
Descriptive summary of allocations per participating hospital. No operational allocation analysis.
The percentage shows this cell's share of all allocations and highlights where the data is concentrated.
No data available for the selected scope.
Shows shares in the table: within the row for matrices, otherwise of the grand total. Applies to count and summable metrics (e.g. total beds), not average, min, or max.
Some analysis settings were adjusted to supported values.
This analysis configuration is not supported.
No data available for the selected scope and period.
This analysis configuration cannot be displayed.
The analysis could not be loaded. Please try again or adjust your filters.
The analysis query failed. Please try again or adjust your filters.
Total grain is not allowed for the time dimension.
No saved views are available yet.
The saved view configuration is invalid. Showing the default analysis instead.
You have not favorited any views yet.
You have not saved any personal views yet.
Monthly allocation counts over the selected period.
Yearly allocation totals as a line chart.
Yearly allocation counts over the selected period, shown as {chart}.
Allocation counts grouped by patient gender.
Allocation counts grouped by urgency level.
Yearly allocation counts stacked by urgency.
{grain} allocation counts by urgency level, shown as {chart}.
Build the result table as rows × columns. Each cell shows the selected metric.
Defines the table row labels and the chart X-axis (categories).
Optional. Splits the data into table columns and chart series (legend entries).
The value shown in each cell and used for chart bars or lines.
How the table and chart are rendered.
Dimension for each row, e.g. month or gender.
Leave empty for a single series. Otherwise each value becomes a column and legend entry.
Granularity for the column dimension when it supports time grouping.
Time bucket for each row, e.g. month or year.
How values of the row dimension are grouped.
Which measure drives the chart. Additional metrics can appear as table columns.
Matrix layouts are available when a column dimension is selected.
Save or discard your analysis changes before editing the view title and description.
Allocation counts from the statistics projection, filtered by scope and period.
Hospital master data snapshot: structure, beds, and allocation-derived metrics per hospital.
Which hospitals are included in the analysis.
Filter hospitals by participation status or compare both groups side by side.
Restrict the allocation set before grouping by rows and columns.
Transport time per hospital distribution (box plot)
This saved view belongs to a different data source. Showing the default analysis for the selected source instead.
Structural hospital metrics such as bed counts are independent of the selected time period. Allocation-derived metrics respect the period filter.
Optional extra columns in the results table. The chart always uses the chart metric above.
Monthly allocation counts over the selected period.
Yearly allocation totals as a line chart.
Allocation counts grouped by patient gender.
Monthly allocation counts split by gender.
Allocation counts grouped by urgency level.
Yearly allocation counts stacked by urgency.
Allocation counts grouped by patient age group.
Which departments handle the most allocations.
Ground versus air transport allocation counts.
Allocations with versus without physician accompaniment.
Allocation counts grouped by secondary indication.
Allocation counts grouped by transport duration bucket in minutes.
Distribution of transport times in minutes per urgency level as a box plot.
Allocation counts as a heatmap of weekday versus time-of-day bucket.
Allocation counts as a heatmap of weekday versus shift bucket.
Allocation counts distributed across hours of the day (0–23).
Resuscitation room and cath lab rates compared for the selected period.
Clinical feature rates compared for the selected period.
Resuscitation room and cath lab rates grouped by gender.
Clinical feature rates grouped by urgency level.
Allocation counts grouped by whether a resuscitation room was requested at registration.
Allocation counts grouped by whether a cath lab was requested at registration.
Allocation counts grouped by CPR case flag.
Allocation counts grouped by ventilation case flag.
Allocation counts grouped by shock case flag.
Which medical specialities handle the most allocations.
Allocation counts grouped by infection flag.
Participating hospital counts grouped by master cohort.
Participating hospital counts grouped by care tier.
Hospital counts by tier split into participating and non-participating.
Participating hospital counts and average beds grouped by size class.
Participating hospital counts in a tier-by-location matrix.
How many allocations participating hospitals handle per care tier.
Distribution of hospital bed counts per care tier as a box plot.
Distribution of allocations per hospital by care tier as a box plot.
Distribution of median transport times per hospital by care tier as a box plot.
Participating hospital counts grouped by location type.
Distribution of hospital bed counts per location type as a box plot.
How many allocations participating hospitals handle per size class.
How many allocations participating hospitals handle per location type.
